extraction.one_line_summary: BJERG Mikkel claimed first pro victory in 31km rolling ITT, beating VINGEGAARD Jonas by 12 seconds despite trailing 12 seconds at first checkpoint.
tactical_patterns: BJERG overturned 12-second deficit to VINGEGAARD after first checkpoint, suggesting superior pacing strategy on climbing/technical sectors.
CAVAGNA Rémi rode unsustainably fast early split at 49kph average, ultimately fading to third after leading at intermediate.
YATES Adam matched CAVAGNA's early pace but UAE pacing protocol proved superior to relying on personal power meter targets.
MAS Enric already hemorrhaged 30 seconds by first checkpoint, confirming TT weakness eliminates him from GC before mountain stages.

rider_signals.BJERG Mikkel: positive: First pro win shows UAE domestique has elite TT capability when given freedom, negative pacing discipline beats raw power
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Second place confirms TT strength but inability to beat teammate-level rider suggests vulnerability if Pogacar were present
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: Fifth on stage and third overall demonstrates 2022 Tour form returning, TT no longer major weakness
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Only one second down at first split shows improved TT form, final placing suggests endurance not power issue
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: Losing 2.5 minutes eliminates GC threat before mountains, confirming TT remains career-limiting weakness
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: Leading early split at 49kph unsustainable, revealing overconfidence when setting tempo without rivals visible
rider_signals.GAUDU David: negative: Dropped over two minutes confirms individual TT remains critical gap despite team's climbing strength
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: neutral: Colombian ITT champion losing 40 seconds to CAVAGNA suggests form not matching palmares expectations
rider_signals.HERREGODTS Rune: positive: Top 10 finish achieves stated goal despite recent crash, showing TT as genuine strength not just climbing
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: neutral: Finishing with main GC group but no standout performance suggests TT parity not advantage over rivals
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: negative: Yellow jersey holder finished 21st at 1:50, losing lead confirms classics specialist cannot defend in stage races with TTs
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: positive: Fourth on stage and second in young rider classification reveals emerging TT capability beyond breakaway specialist reputation
